mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate compresses the equity discount rate and lowers the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the zero lower bound regime persists.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns during ZIRP periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional 65.5% positive-day base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over the trailing two months signals an accelerating labor market recovery that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% as of 2020-12-01, down from pandemic peak
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 20d positive-return rate not statistically distinguishable from unconditional base rate (65.5%), or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a self-sustaining, income-financed consumer demand expansion rather than transitory stimulus-driven spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. Retail sales strength without payroll growth is treated as unsustainable and does not trigger the signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS m/m growth > 1% AND PAYEMS m/m growth > 0.3% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 540,915M and PAYEMS at 142,624K as of Dec 2020 vintage
regime_hint: consumer-led recovery with improving labor market
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances underperform single-series RSAFS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or 60d forward positive-return rate in triggered periods falls below the unconditional base rate.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growing above 1% month-over-month for two consecutive months signals a strengthening supply-side recovery that boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing a short-horizon positive S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO m/m growth > 1.0% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 105.72 vs revised 98.36 as of Dec 2020, indicating rapid post-COVID rebound
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO surges are not significantly different from non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05), or effect reverses sign in more than one out of three rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-pandemic recovery with near-zero policy rates, simultaneous strength in housing starts (HOUST) and ultra-low FEDFUNDS jointly amplify equity returns via a housing-wealth-effect and cheap-credit channel that is materially weaker or absent outside this specific regime, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,543-1,547 thousand units and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of late 2020
regime_hint: post-pandemic recovery under zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS signal fails to outperform the HOUST-only single-series signal in out-of-sample testing, or shows no significant regime interaction effect (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10) when the ZIRP regime ends.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustaining a level above 1,500 thousand units for three consecutive months signals durable demand strength in a large consumption-adjacent sector, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1500 thousand units for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,543-1,547 thousand units as of Nov 2020 vintage
failure_condition: Forward 20d positive-return rate in triggered periods is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional base rate, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls carries immediate informational content for near-term risk sentiment, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days whenever payroll growth is positive, independent of any threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,624K as of Dec 2020 vintage
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints do not exceed those following negative prints by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) across the full 2005-2024 sample.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation accelerates above 2% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains pinned below 0.25%, deeply negative real interest rates incentivize reach-for-yield behavior into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this effect requires the joint condition of rising inflation and pinned policy rate and does not hold if either condition is absent.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value 261.775 revised to 262.045 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of Dec 2020
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Once CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, if forward 20d returns turn negative in more than 40% of triggered instances or the interaction term loses significance (p>0.10) relative to a CPI-only model, the hypothesis is rejected.
